description_long: This guard stone features a robust square base with a circular raised platform, surrounded by four stout pillars at each corner. Atop the central pedestal stands a stylized statue of a bearded figure holding a sword aloft, with a flowing cape and detailed facial features. The pedestal itself is adorned with engraved plaques on two sides, likely for commemorative or informational purposes. The structure is monolithic and appears to be carved from a single block of stone, with architectural arches decorating the base. The overall design suggests a blend of functional barrier and decorative monument, suitable for marking important locations or entrances. The surface is uniformly textured, with no visible color variation, and the form is both imposing and ornamental.
description: A monumental guard stone with a square base, circular platform, four pillars, and a statue of a bearded figure holding a sword. Engraved plaques and decorative arches enhance its imposing, commemorative appearance.
description_view_0: Front view shows the statue facing forward, with the four pillars and the circular platform clearly visible.
description_view_1: Rear view reveals the back of the statue and a prominent engraved plaque on the pedestal, with the cape flowing to the side.
description_view_2: Side view highlights the symmetry of the pillars and the unadorned side of the pedestal, with the statue in profile.
description_view_3: Opposite front view displays the statue facing forward, another engraved plaque, and the full arrangement of pillars and arches.
attribute_location: Typically placed at the entrance to parks, plazas, or historical sites, often flanking pathways or marking significant boundaries in urban or heritage settings.
attribute_installation_orientation: Fully above ground, upright, and firmly anchored to prevent movement or displacement.
attribute_wear_patterns: Likely to show chipping and smoothing on corners and edges from incidental contact, as well as surface erosion and discoloration from prolonged exposure to weather. The top surfaces and statue may exhibit more pronounced weathering, while the base may show dirt accumulation.
attribute_historical_markings: Engraved plaques with inscriptions are present on at least two sides of the pedestal, featuring stylized text or commemorative information.
